\boxed{B}
**Clinical Reasoning:** The occurrence of white hair in a girl and her brother (who shares the same family background but lives elsewhere) suggests a hereditary pattern. Since only a small number of classmates show the trait and none outside the village do, it points toward a rare autosomal recessive or dominant inheritance rather than environmental, nutritional, or infection-related causes. White hair is known to sometimes follow Mendelian patterns, especially when observed in siblings.
